brief_justification: Arrives at correct answer (B) with valid mechanistic reasoning referencing Prigogine and the canonical river example; multi-level analysis traces why each option fails at the structural level — what each concept requires vs. what the examples provide, plus second-order note that structure exists because of flow not despite it; shows 'discrimination logic' framing and structured elimination but no explicit reflection on own reasoning biases or method; performs basic sanity check ('stop the flow, the structure vanishes') but no counterfactual or boundary-condition testing; presents conclusions with high confidence, though does honestly acknowledge emergence is 'real in all three examples'; systematically evaluates all four options with specific mechanistic rejection reasons for each; full derivation chain — concept definition → mismatch diagnosis → positive confirmation — reproducible by third party; avoids conflating autopoiesis with dissipative structure (the core failure mode implied by the negative gate) but never explicitly names why that conflation is tempting or wrong; avoids static system assumption and external-control-sufficiency failure modes but does not explicitly call them out; response is structured (elimination branches per option) but remains essentially linear rather than mapping to the topology's gates, M-node reflection loop, or FREEFORM exit paths
